PIC1.DAX:001:000.notes: The maintainer explicitly requested the supplied 2026-07-21 treasure still for every treasure scene. PIC1-PIC6 block 001 source frames are byte-identical (SHA-256 1e04224c78b230c67b72d58dd3f58e3fcfb3ee8c164aa95a7d44b722b12440fb), so one reviewed 1254x1254 replacement is deliberately shared across all six archive identities. It preserves the dominant diagonal blade, helmet, coins, parchment, bottle and jewel composition. The normal-picture retained layer uses the shared ovr030 panel-replacement lifecycle.

BODY2.DAX:000:000.notes: Source-locked attempt 2 local edit remains too broad; gold fixtures, purple emblem, pauldrons, magenta cloth and turquoise clasp are materially oversized. Two-attempt gate reached; preserved and escalated for human QC.

BODY2.DAX:001:000.notes: Correct person-reference attempt and localized edit both failed source lock: altered hands/arm crop, central plate and diagonal object geometry, purple cloth distribution, and hard black cutouts. Two-attempt gate reached; human QC and shared HEAD/BODY lighting contract required.

PIC1.DAX:029:000.notes: The maintainer supplied the replacement 10_16_59 AM four-frame set and explicitly requested integration on 2026-07-15. Filename order (1)-(4) maps to DAX frames 0-3 and supersedes the earlier 09_58_52/09_58_53 set. The generic normal-picture retained layer advances with each original animation frame. Initial live replay exposed a stale camp layer during combat. BattleSetup was corrected to retire all retained gameplay picture layers inside the stopped combat update batch. Post-fix native-rate replay passed: last camp frame 40.67s, first complete combat frame 41.00s, no stale overlay, fallback flash, blank panel, or partial combat frame.

HEAD2.DAX:003:000.notes: The maintainer approved the combined Tiverton innkeeper portrait in Telegram on 2026-07-15. HEAD2 block 003 and BODY2 block 003 were split at the exact 11:5/11:6 engine seam, then tested in the isolated Full Auto runtime. Catalog tracing confirmed both identities loaded. Original neck width was 12 logical pixels; HD was 11.95 logical pixels (0.42% difference), passing the +/-1 logical pixel tolerance. Live seam, lighting, skin continuity and panel containment passed.
